title: Peltogyne riedeliana
intro: Peltogyne riedeliana, also known as Brazilian ironwood or riodinha, is a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae, commonly found in the tropical forests of South America. Traditionally, Peltogyne riedeliana has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and wounds, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Peltogyne riedeliana include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Peltogyne riedeliana is often used to prepare infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are commonly consumed to treat various health conditions.
taxonomy: Peltogyne riedeliana is classified as a species of tree in the family Fabaceae. This plant belongs to the genus Peltogyne, which is a group of flowering plants native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. Peltogyne riedeliana is further classified as a member of the subfamily Faboideae, which is one of the largest and most diverse subfamilies within the Fabaceae family. The plant is also known to be a part of the order Fabales, which includes various flowering plants and trees. Peltogyne riedeliana is considered to be an important species in its native habitats due to its unique characteristics and adaptations.
